title: Rendering Model For Auth
summary: Authenticated pages are request-time rendered because session cookies are request-time data.
purpose: Teams need to know whether protected pages are static, dynamic, SSR, or client-rendered.
rule: Treat protected pages and layouts that read cookies or current-session data as dynamic request-time routes. Public marketing and documentation pages can remain static. Do not move auth checks to client rendering to preserve static generation.
whenToUse: Building protected layouts that call getCurrentSession.
Rendering app shells that depend on roles, account context, or preferences.
Explaining performance tradeoffs in architecture review.
whenNotToUse: Do not make a protected page static if its content depends on the current user.
Do not fetch sensitive user data only after hydration.
Do not cache personalized responses globally.
Do not read cookies in a shared root layout that wraps public pages unless all children must become dynamic.

recommended.title: Dynamic protected layout

avoid.title: Client-only auth gate

why: Next cookies are request-time data. Reading cookies or deriving a current session means the route cannot be fully static for every user. That is the correct tradeoff for protected product surfaces: security and correctness come before static rendering. Keep public pages static, keep authenticated surfaces dynamic, and keep expensive feature data fetching scoped to the routes that need it.
checklist: Protected layouts read session data on the server.
Public routes stay outside auth-dependent layouts.
Personalized data is not globally cached.
Client components receive only safe serialized session data.
Route-level auth and data-level auth both exist.
